message:
test(config,ensemble): eliminate all 0% functions in config and ensemble packages (bd-1fjt9)

Config: IncrementCommand/Session/Prompt, ProficiencyConfigPath, UpsertPaletteState,
SetProjectsBase, Reset — 82.5% → 84.2%. Ensemble: DedupeFindingsWithProvenance,
NewModeOutputCache — 88.1% → 88.2%. Zero 0% functions remain in either package.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1edma","title":"Task: Implement ntm --robot-jfp-* wrapper","description":"## Overview
Implement robot wrapper commands that proxy to `jfp` with JSON output.

## Responsibilities
- Call `jfp <cmd> --json` (or rely on non-TTY JSON) and validate schema
- Normalize output into NTM robot envelope when appropriate
- Map jfp exit codes to NTM error codes (NOT_FOUND, INVALID_FLAG, DEPENDENCY_MISSING)
- Support `--project` installs when requested

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each wrapper returns stable JSON
- Errors include actionable hints (install jfp, invalid id, etc.)
- No prompts in robot mode","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:11:49.935655444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:43.382429345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:43.382347771Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm --robot-jfp-* wrapper commands: status, list, search, show, suggest, installed, categories, tags, bundles. All commands return stable JSON with proper error codes (DEPENDENCY_MISSING, NOT_FOUND, INVALID_FLAG) and actionable hints.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1edma","depends_on_id":"bd-1q6xh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1edma","depends_on_id":"bd-3kfv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1es69","title":"process_triage: Implement background health monitoring loop","description":"Create background goroutine that monitors agent health via pt.

## Requirements

1. Monitor all agent panes continuously
2. Call pt with agent PIDs at configured interval
3. Track classification history
4. Trigger alerts on threshold breach

## Implementation

```go
package pt

type HealthMonitor struct {
    config      *config.ProcessTriageConfig
    pidMap      *rano.PIDMap  // reuse from rano integration
    states      map[string]*AgentState  // pane -> state
    alertCh     chan Alert
    stopCh      chan struct{}
}

type AgentState struct {
    Pane           string
    Classification string  // useful, waiting, idle, stuck, zombie
    Since          time.Time
    History        []ClassificationEvent
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) Start() {
    go m.monitorLoop()
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) monitorLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(m.config.CheckInterval) * time.Second)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.checkAll()
        case <-m.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) checkAll() {
    for pane, pid := range m.pidMap.GetAll() {
        classification := m.classify(pid)
        m.updateState(pane, classification)
        m.checkAlerts(pane)
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/pt/monitor.go

## Integration with rano
If rano enabled, include network activity in classification:
- Network activity → likely waiting, not stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:56.937143241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094897305Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094809931Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1h5xe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1sa5u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1fl0i","title":"Task: Unit tests for privacy mode gating","description":"## Scope
Add unit tests to ensure privacy mode prevents persistence.

## Cases
- Privacy OFF → persistence allowed.
- Privacy ON → persistence blocked (and returns a deterministic error).
- Privacy ON + explicit override → allowed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are hermetic and do not write outside temp dirs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:42.361516233Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:50.397401605Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:50.397268817Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fl0i","depends_on_id":"bd-26364","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fl0i","depends_on_id":"bd-35sy7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fmug","title":"Feature: Robot output format selector (json|toon|auto)","description":"## Overview
Allow robot mode to emit JSON or TOON with a single flag and config default.

## Interface
- Flag: `--robot-format=json|toon|auto`
- Config: `robot.format = json|toon|auto`
- Env: `NTM_ROBOT_FORMAT` overrides config

## Behavior
- `json`: current behavior (canonical)
- `toon`: emit TOON (if encoder available; otherwise error with hint)
- `auto`: prefer TOON if available, else JSON

## Compatibility
- `--robot-terse` remains a separate single‑line output and is **not** affected by `--robot-format`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flag + config plumbed through root
- Format chosen before output serialization
- Error on `toon` when encoder unavailable
- `auto` gracefully falls back to JSON","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:01.582718931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:30:45.739670785Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:30:45.739596154Z","close_reason":"Implemented robot output format selector with --robot-format flag (json|toon|auto), NTM_ROBOT_FORMAT env var support, and global OutputFormat variable. Added tests for format selector functionality.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fmug","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fmug","depends_on_id":"bd-2h4bq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
